result:
**Goal: Add a genetic algorithm evolution mode** that breeds Life-like rulesets through natural selection.

Users would start a population of random B/S rulesets, each running on its own small grid simultaneously. After N generations, a fitness function scores each ruleset (e.g., population stability, longevity, pattern diversity), the top performers reproduce with mutation, and the next generation of rulesets runs. Users watch rulesets evolve in real-time across a tiled multi-grid display, can tune mutation rate and fitness criteria, and "adopt" any evolved ruleset into the main simulator.

This builds naturally on the existing rule editor, comparison mode, and race mode infrastructure while adding a genuinely novel dimension to the project.
